data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 23, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 23, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: SGX Opens Lower As Global Jitters Spill Over
data.description:
Singapore equities opened on a softer note on Friday, tracking overnight losses on Wall Street as geopolitical tensions and uneven corporate earnings weighed on sentiment.

The benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) fell 27.95 points or 0.57% to 4,916.16 as at 9.17am, with decliners outpacing advancers 120 to 104. Trading volume stood at 181.99 million shares valued at S$300.38 million.

The weaker start followed a decline in US markets, where all three major indices ended lower amid fading optimism over a resolution to the Iran conflict. Oil prices hovering near US$100 a barrel have also kept inflation concerns elevated, adding pressure to equities globally.

Market sentiment remained cautious after tensions escalated in the Strait of Hormuz and reports of air defence activity in Iran, developments that have heightened risk-off positioning among investors. Technology stocks were among the biggest drags in the US, further dampening appetite for equities across the region.

In derivatives trading, FTSE China A50 Index Futures for April were at 15,650.00, while Nikkei 225 Index Futures for June stood at 59,475.00, indicating a mixed regional outlook.

Despite the softer tone, broader fundamentals remain supported by resilient earnings trends, although near-term volatility is expected to persist as investors navigate geopolitical risks and shifting expectations around inflation and global growth.

data.title: Japan’s Nikkei Crosses 60,000 Level For The First Time
data.description:
Japan’s Nikkei share average crossed the 60,000 level for the first time on Thursday (Apr 23), as SoftBank Group and other technology stocks gained and risk sentiment improved after United States President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran.

The Nikkei was last up 0.34 per cent at 59,790, as of 12.10pm (8.10pm, Singapore time). The broader Topix was down 0.7 per cent at 3,718.59.

Chip-related shares rose, with Advantest and Tokyo Electron up 2.65 per cent and 0.81 per cent, respectively.

Technology investor SoftBank Group jumped 8.9 per cent.

Fibre optic cable maker Fujikura gained 1 per cent.

Trump said the indefinite extension of the ceasefire followed a request by Pakistani mediators. However, the US Navy’s blockade of Iranian ports remained in effect, and Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

Of all the Nikkei components, 42 stocks rose and 158 fell, with five trading flat.

Reuters

data.title: US Stocks Slip As Iran Tensions And Earnings Weigh
data.description:
U.S. stocks closed lower on Thursday as escalating tensions involving Iran and uneven corporate earnings dampened investor sentiment, reversing earlier stability in a choppy trading session.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 179.71 points or 0.36% to 49,310.32, while the S&P 500 declined 0.41% to 7,108.40. The Nasdaq Composite led losses, dropping 0.89% to 24,438.50, weighed down by weakness in technology stocks.

Markets had previously rallied on expectations of a resolution to the Iran conflict, but that optimism has faded. Oil prices hovering near US$100 a barrel have also kept inflation concerns firmly in focus, adding pressure to equities.

Tensions intensified after Iran tightened its grip on the Strait of Hormuz and reports emerged of air defence systems being activated following drone activity. Stocks weakened further after news that Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf had stepped down from the negotiating team, while reports of air attacks pushed oil prices higher.

“We’re playing musical chairs between earnings season and these war headlines that are not likely to be that great,” said Jay Hatfield, CEO and CIO of Infrastructure Capital Advisors in New York.
“We had a big run, and there are people looking to take some exposure off, and using the war as an excuse is not a bad excuse.”

Technology stocks were the biggest drag, with the S&P 500 tech index falling 1.47%. IBM slid 8.25% after slower software revenue growth, while ServiceNow tumbled 17.75% due to delays in government deals. The declines reignited concerns that artificial intelligence could disrupt traditional software business models, sending the software and services index down 5.09%, its steepest fall since late January.

Tesla dropped 3.56% after raising its annual spending plan to more than US$25 billion, while Avis Budget plunged 48.38%, marking its sharpest two-day fall on record following a recent surge.

In contrast, Texas Instruments surged 19.43%, its biggest daily gain in over 25 years, after issuing an upbeat forecast for the second quarter.

Despite the volatility, earnings season has remained broadly supportive, with more than 82% of reporting companies beating expectations so far, and overall earnings growth estimated at 15.6%, according to LSEG data.

Economic data showed initial jobless claims rose only marginally last week, suggesting a still-resilient labour market, though rising costs linked to the conflict could weigh on the broader economy.

Trading volume stood at 17.41 billion shares, slightly below the recent average, while declining stocks outpaced advancers across both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq.

Reuters

data.title: China’s Yuan Weakens As Dollar Regains Strength Amid Ceasefire
data.description:
The central parity rate of the Chinese currency renminbi, or the yuan, weakened 15 pips to 6.865 against the U.S. dollar Thursday, according to the China Foreign Exchange Trade System.

In China’s spot foreign exchange market, the yuan is allowed to rise or fall by 2 percent from the central parity rate each trading day.

The central parity rate of the yuan against the U.S. dollar is based on a weighted average of prices offered by market makers before the opening of the interbank market each business day.

US President Donald Trump has announced an indefinite ceasefire in the Middle East, easing tensions in the region, which also saw oil prices retreating.

data.title: Bursa Extends Gains At Midday As FBM KLCI Rises 0.49%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its positive momentum at midday, with the FBM KLCI rising 0.49% to 1,718.70 as broader market sentiment stayed firm across most indices.

The FBM 70 outperformed, climbing 0.79% to 17,976.15, while the FBM Small Cap index added 0.32% to 12,521.74. The FBM Emas rose 0.51% to 12,682.84 and the FBM Emas Shariah gained 0.52% to 1,024.25, reflecting broad-based buying interest across mid and large-cap counters.

Trading activity remained heavy with turnover concentrated in several high-volume stocks, led by AMS which saw 870.71 million shares traded as it held at 29 sen after an earlier decline. ZETRIX eased 0.5 sen to 85.5 sen while AirAsia X fell 7 sen to RM1.23, making it among the most actively traded counters during the session.

On the gainers’ board, MPI led advances, rising RM0.98 to RM36.62, followed by Kawan Renergy which added RM0.30 to RM5.99. HLFG also gained RM0.16 to RM19.30, contributing to the positive tone among selected financial and industrial names.

On the decliners’ list, Nestle slipped RM0.44 to RM98.30 while F&N eased RM0.36 to RM30.56. UTDPLT dropped RM0.32 to RM33.40 and MISC declined RM0.22 to RM8.08, reflecting mild profit taking in defensive and plantation-linked stocks.

data.title: Stock Today: MR D.I.Y. Jumps 4% As Bullish Momentum Builds
data.description:
MR D.I.Y. Group (M) Bhd extended its upward momentum, in line with analysts’ bullish technical view, as the stock climbed to RM1.67 at 4.42 pm, up seven sen or 4.38% from its last done price of RM1.60.

RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) recently highlighted the counter as a near-term trading opportunity, noting that the stock had entered a bullish phase after breaking above the RM1.60 resistance level on strong volume, signalling growing buying interest.

During the session, MR D.I.Y. traded between RM1.59 and RM1.68 after opening at RM1.60, with total volume reaching 10.06 million shares. The counter’s latest price places it within reach of the next resistance band identified by analysts.

RHB Research said the strengthening price action and rising participation suggest momentum could be sustained in the near term, with potential upside towards RM1.70 and RM1.80 if buying interest continues to build.

The research house, however, cautioned that a fall below the RM1.50 support level could invalidate the bullish setup and trigger a correction.

The stock’s recent performance reflects improving sentiment around the consumer retail segment, with technical indicators pointing to renewed investor interest following the breakout above key resistance levels.

data.title: Stock Picks: Telekom And Top Glove
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Telekom Malaysia Bhd and Top Glove Corp Bhd as potential trading opportunities, citing improving technical momentum and the likelihood of bullish breakouts for both counters.

The research house said Telekom Malaysia is attempting to stage a breakout after testing its immediate resistance with a bullish candlestick pattern. The stock has been consolidating below the RM7.10 level but has moved above its 21-day simple moving average, indicating strengthening upward momentum.

RHB Research noted that a successful breach above the RM7.10 resistance could see Telekom Malaysia advance towards RM7.35, with further upside to RM7.60. However, it cautioned that a drop below the RM6.85 support level may signal a resumption of its correction phase.

Meanwhile, Top Glove is seen extending its counter-trend rebound, supported by a series of higher low candlestick formations that point to building bullish momentum. The research house said the improving price structure suggests increasing buying interest in the stock.

It added that a breakout above the RM0.76 resistance level could attract fresh momentum, potentially driving the counter towards RM0.81 and subsequently RM0.86. On the downside, a fall below RM0.71 would indicate a continuation of the corrective trend.

RHB Research said both counters are showing signs of strengthening technical setups, with price action suggesting near-term upside potential if key resistance levels are cleared.

data.title: TA Securities Pegs Land & General At 20 Sen On Strong Earnings Upside
data.description:
TA Securities Sdn Bhd has pegged an indicative fair value of 20 sen for Land & General Bhd (L&G), underpinned by expectations of sharp earnings rebound and improving sales visibility over the next three years.

The research house projects L&G’s profit to expand significantly to between RM52 million and RM83 million for FY26-FY28 compared to RM29.5 million in FY25, driven by a solid pipeline of unbilled sales and upcoming project launches.

TA Securities highlighted that L&G’s unbilled sales stand at RM637 million, providing near-term earnings support, while new sales are forecast at RM450 million in FY26, RM513 million in FY27 and RM1.1 billion in FY28.

TA Securities also highlighted L&G’s attractive valuation, noting the stock is trading at 6.9 times CY26 earnings and 5.4 times CY27 earnings, levels seen as inexpensive relative to sector peers.

“On dividends, the group is expected to deliver yields exceeding 6%, assuming a 50% payout ratio, with projected dividends of 0.9 sen to 1.4 sen per share over FY26-FY28. This compares favourably with the sector’s average yield of 4.4% to 5.5%,” the research house said.

TA Securities explained that the 20 sen valuation is based on a target price-to-book multiple of 0.5 times, in line with small-cap property stocks.

Meanwhile, TA Securities cautioned that rebuilding investor confidence may take time following L&G’s past financial challenges, even though prospects are improving.

“Nonetheless, FY26 is seen as a potential inflection point, supported by earnings recovery and renewed investor relations efforts,” it said.

The stock is currently not rated by the research house.

data.title: Bursa Closes Higher As Ceasefire Boosts Sentiment
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended higher on April 23, supported by selective buying in banking and telecommunications heavyweights amid improved investor sentiment following the extended ceasefire in West Asia.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 11.31 points, or 0.66%, to close at 1,721.70, up from 1,710.39 in the previous session.

Broader market indices also advanced, with the FBM 70 gaining 180.98 points (1.01%) to 18,016.25, while the FBM Emas and FBM Shariah indices rose 88.14 points (0.7%) and 84.17 points (0.67%) to 12,706.16 and 12,565.36, respectively, reflecting a generally positive market tone.

Trading activity strengthened, with total turnover increasing to 3.48 billion units worth RM3.1 billion compared to 3.19 billion units valued at RM2.69 billion in the previous session.

Among the most active counters, Zetrix AI Bhd edged up 0.5 sen to 86.5 sen on heavy volume of 131.7 million shares, while AMS Advanced Material Bhd added 1.5 sen to 30.5 sen in active trade following its market debut.

Velesto Energy Bhd slipped one sen to 32 sen, while AirAsia X Bhd fell seven sen to RM1.23. Bumi Armada Bhd, however, rose three sen to 41.5 sen.

The firmer close reflects cautious optimism in the market, with investors selectively accumulating key heavyweights amid easing geopolitical tensions.

data.title: STI Dips At Open Despite Wall Street Record Rally
data.description:
Singapore equities opened slightly lower on Thursday, tracking cautious regional sentiment despite a strong overnight lead from Wall Street.

The benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) slipped 14.87 points, or 0.3%, to 4,987.85 as at 9.07am, with decliners outpacing advancers at 95 to 123. Total trading volume stood at 148.02 million shares valued at S$170.28 million.

The softer start comes after US markets climbed to fresh record highs overnight, driven by optimism following the extension of the Iran ceasefire and a solid start to the earnings season.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.69% to 49,490.03, the S&P 500 gained 1.05% to 7,137.90 while the Nasdaq Composite outperformed with a 1.64% jump to 24,657.57.

Investor sentiment has been supported by expectations of easing geopolitical tensions after US President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran indefinitely following mediation efforts by Pakistan. However, uncertainties remain as disruptions continue in the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil supply route.

Oil-related concerns continue to linger, with prices hovering near US$100 per barrel, raising risks of inflationary pressures that could affect global growth.

Technology stocks led gains in the US, supported by strong earnings momentum, with first-quarter earnings growth tracking around 14%. Market breadth also remained positive, reflecting continued investor confidence.

Back in Singapore, derivatives trading showed mixed signals, with MSCI Singapore Index Futures at 448.40, while regional futures including Nikkei 225 Index Futures stood at 60,025.00.

data.title: Gainers Outpace Losers On Bursa As PetChem, MPI Lead Active Movers
data.description:
Stock movements on Bursa Malaysia ended broadly higher, with gainers outpacing losers 706 to 485, reflecting improved market sentiment despite a sizeable number of inactive counters.

A total of 562 stocks closed unchanged, while 1,018 counters were untraded and 43 suspended, pointing to selective buying interest across the market.

Among the top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI) rose 70 sen to RM36.34, leading the pack, followed by Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd which added 48 sen to RM32.86.

Heavyweight Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd climbed 33 sen to RM5.55 on strong trading volume of 177.1 million shares, while Kelington Group Bhd (KGB) gained 29 sen to RM5.98. Allianz Malaysia Bhd also edged up 28 sen to RM21.28.

On the downside, Nestle (M) Bhd led decliners, falling 74 sen to RM98, followed by United Plantations Bhd which slipped 32 sen to RM33.40.

Other notable losers included Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, down 22 sen to RM30.70, Hong Leong Industries Bhd which shed 20 sen to RM17.38 and MISC Bhd, also lower by 20 sen to RM8.10.

Overall, the market’s positive breadth suggests cautious optimism, with buying concentrated in selected heavyweights and actively traded counters.

data.title: IPO Debut: AMS Advanced Closes At 30.5 Sen After Choppy ACE Market Debut
data.description:
AMS Advanced Material Bhd closed its ACE Market debut at 30.5 sen on April 23, staging a modest recovery from a weak start despite heavy selling pressure in early trade.

The stock slipped below its IPO price of 29 sen shortly after the opening bell, trading at 28.5 sen and hitting an intraday low of 28 sen as selling outweighed buying interest. It later rebounded to 29 sen by midday before edging higher into the close.

Trading activity was brisk, with more than 105.8 million shares changing hands by the end of the session compared to around 87.1 million shares traded at midday.

Despite the subdued debut, the company enters the market with growth prospects tied to a recovery in the semiconductor sector. The company raised RM32.8 million from its IPO, with proceeds earmarked mainly for capacity expansion and product development.

AMS Advanced was listed on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia via the issuance of 113 million new shares at 29 sen each and an offer for sale of 47 million existing shares to selected investors.

The muted first-day performance reflects cautious investor sentiment, even as the company positions itself to ride on improving demand in the semiconductor space.

data.title: Bearish Sentiment Deepens For HSIF, Support Seen At 25,100
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) maintained a bearish stance on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), advising traders to keep their short positions as negative momentum continues to build.

The research house said HSIF extended its correction on Thursday, falling 240 points to close at 25,921 points. The contract opened at 26,265 points and briefly touched a high of 26,266 points before selling pressure intensified, dragging it down to an intraday low of 25,839 points.

Losses continued into the evening session, with the index shedding a further 119 points to last trade at 25,802 points, reflecting persistent downside pressure.

RHB Research noted that the latest price action, alongside a declining relative strength index, signals that bearish momentum is gaining pace amid a broader risk-off sentiment.

It added that the index is likely to drift lower, with immediate support seen at 25,100 points, followed by 24,500 points. On the upside, resistance is expected to remain firm at 26,600 points, with a stronger ceiling at 27,200 points.

Given the prevailing trend, the research house said traders should maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, while keeping a stop-loss level at 26,600 points to manage risks.

data.title: Market Movement Ends Mixed As Losers Edge Ahead
data.description:
The stock market movement closed on a cautious note, with decliners narrowly outpacing gainers as weakness in selected heavyweights offset pockets of late-session buying interest.

Market breadth remained negative, with 584 losers versus 565 gainers, while 596 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,014 counters were untraded and 35 suspended, reflecting a largely subdued trading environment.

Losses among key blue chips dragged on overall market performance, led by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd, which fell sharply, alongside financial heavyweight Hong Leong Bank Bhd and conglomerate Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd.

Consumer names were also under pressure, with Heineken Malaysia Bhd and Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd retreating amid mild profit-taking.

On the upside, defensive and consumer staples counters helped cushion the broader market. Nestlé (M) Bhd led gainers, followed by Petronas Dagangan Bhd, reflecting continued demand for resilient earnings plays.

Industrial and technology-linked counters also saw selective interest, with UWC Bhd among the most actively traded gainers, while Hong Leong Industries Bhd and plantation player Batu Kawan Bhd posted modest gains.

Despite the near-balanced market breadth, the overall tone remained cautious, with investors rotating into defensives while trimming positions in cyclical and high-beta names.

Analysts said the market is likely to stay range-bound in the near term, with sentiment hinging on external cues and the performance of heavyweight stocks, which continue to dictate overall direction.

data.title: HSIF Slips Below 26,600 Resistance, Short Bias Kept
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading outlook on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), advising traders to hold short positions after the index retreated sharply from key resistance levels.

The research house said HSIF fell 331 points to close at 26,161 points, after opening at 26,487 points and sliding to an intraday low of 26,085 points before the close. In after-hours trading, the index edged up slightly by 8 points to 26,169 points.

RHB Research noted that the rejection at the 26,600-point level signals a return of bearish momentum, suggesting sellers have regained control of the market. It added that the latest pullback indicates potential price weakness, supported by a declining Relative Strength Index.

The firm expects the index to trade within a range in the near term before testing immediate support at 25,100 points. A further downside could see HSIF moving towards the next support level at 24,500 points.

Traders were advised to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage risk.

On the upside, RHB Research identified 26,600 points as immediate resistance, followed by a higher level at 27,200 points, as the market continues to navigate near-term volatility.

data.title: Stock Today: Inari Climbs 1.68% As Bullish Breakout Drives Upside
data.description:
Inari Amertron Bhd rose 1.68% to RM1.82 as at 2.49 pm, supported by sustained buying interest following a recent technical breakout that signalled renewed upside momentum.

The stock opened at RM1.81 and traded between RM1.80 and RM1.84 during the session, hovering near its intraday high with about 15.02 million shares changing hands. Market activity remained firm with buy orders slightly outweighing sell orders, indicating continued accumulation.

The counter’s strength follows a bullish breakout above the RM1.75 resistance level highlighted by RHB Investment Bank, which said the stock could extend its gains towards RM1.90 and RM2.05 if momentum persists. The research house added that the positive setup is supported by improving volume trends and momentum indicators.

Sentiment has also been underpinned by improving medium-term prospects, with both RHB and Hong Leong Investment Bank maintaining positive calls on the stock. Analysts said while FY26 may remain soft due to weaker radio frequency demand and foreign exchange pressures, a stronger recovery is expected in FY27 driven by a new smartphone cycle and higher RF content.

They added that additional growth could come from datacom photonics, as demand for AI-driven optical connectivity and data centre applications accelerates, providing another earnings driver for the group.

Despite near-term headwinds, analysts said valuation remains attractive, supported by the group’s net cash position and improving earnings visibility, with risks tied to weaker smartphone demand and currency fluctuations.

data.title: Stock Today: Genting Rises Amid Ongoing Refinancing Activities
data.description:
Genting Bhd rose 0.79% to RM2.55 as at 11.25 am, with 9.13 million shares traded, tracking steady buying interest amid a positive tone in its capital management outlook and ongoing refinancing activities in the international debt market.

The counter opened at RM2.53, moved between RM2.53 and RM2.57 before settling near its intraday high, with buying support slightly outweighing selling pressure as investors monitored developments around its US dollar-denominated perpetual bond issuance.

Sentiment around the stock was shaped by a CreditSights report stating that Genting is tapping the international debt market with proposed perpetual securities under a refinancing strategy aimed at managing upcoming obligations. The group is marketing two tranches of subordinated perpetual bonds with initial price thoughts of 7.95% and 8.625%, with proceeds expected to be used largely to refinance its US$1.5 billion bond due in January 2027.

CreditSights noted that final pricing could tighten from initial guidance, reflecting investor demand and relative value versus regional peers, while also advising participation in both the new issuance and related tender offer.

The refinancing move comes as Genting navigates elevated capital expenditure and higher leverage linked to expansion initiatives and stake-related investments, although the group continues to be supported by diversified earnings across gaming, plantations, power and oil and gas.

Analysts also highlighted that about 82% of Genting’s revenue is derived from gaming operations across Singapore, Malaysia, the United Kingdom and the United States, providing a stable core cash flow base despite ongoing expansion pressures.

data.title: Stock Picks: Sime Darby Property And QES Group
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Sime Darby Property Bhd and QES Group Bhd as stocks with near-term upside potential, citing bullish technical breakouts supported by strong momentum.

The research house said Sime Darby Property is poised to extend its gains after breaking above the RM1.35 resistance level with strong trading volume. It noted that the stock formed a bullish Marubozu candlestick, signalling firm buying interest and positive momentum.

RHB Research expects the counter to test the next resistance levels at RM1.43 and RM1.51 if the upward trend continues. However, it cautioned that a drop below the RM1.27 support level could weaken sentiment and trigger a reversal.

Meanwhile, QES Group has also entered a bullish phase after closing above the RM0.42 resistance level. The stock registered a “higher high” candlestick pattern, indicating strengthening upward momentum.

The research house said QES is likely to target the next resistance levels at RM0.45 and RM0.48, supported by sustained buying interest. On the downside, it flagged RM0.39 as a key support level, with a breach potentially leading to renewed selling pressure.

RHB Research noted that both counters are currently riding on positive technical setups, with momentum-driven trading expected to guide near-term price movements.

data.title: Wall Street Hits Records As Ceasefire Hopes Lift Sentiment
data.description:
US stocks climbed to fresh highs on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing at record levels, as investors responded positively to an extension of the Iran ceasefire alongside a strong start to the earnings season.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 340.65 points or 0.69% to 49,490.03, while the S&P 500 gained 73.89 points or 1.05% to 7,137.90. The Nasdaq Composite outperformed, advancing 397.60 points or 1.64% to end at 24,657.57.

The rally came after US President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran indefinitely following mediation efforts by Pakistan, although tensions remain unresolved as the US naval blockade of Iranian ports continues and disruptions persist in the Strait of Hormuz.

The waterway, which accounts for about 20% of global oil supply, remains a key uncertainty for markets. Iran’s parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf said a full ceasefire would only be meaningful if the blockade is lifted.

Markets have been buoyed in recent weeks by expectations of a possible de-escalation, even as risks tied to energy supply and inflation linger with oil prices hovering near US$100 per barrel.

“Everyone’s kind of sick of it… clearly, the market is looking for a beneficial outcome or some kind of decent outcome here,” said Stephen Massocca, senior vice president at Wedbush Securities in San Francisco.

“Earnings have been good – now, will they continue to be good if we continue to be at war – it’s going to lose a little bit of its oomph. That said, in my world, there’s still tremendous value, there’s a lot of really cheap stuff out there.”

Technology stocks led gains, with the S&P 500 tech index rising 2.31%, supported by chipmakers. Micron Technology surged 8.48% to a record close, while the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index extended its rally to a sixteenth straight session.

Corporate earnings continued to underpin sentiment, with first quarter growth tracking around 14%, according to LSEG data. GE Vernova jumped 13.75% after raising its annual revenue forecast, while Boston Scientific gained 8.99% on strong quarterly results. Boeing climbed 5.53% after posting a smaller-than-expected loss.

However, not all stocks advanced. United Airlines fell 5.58% after guiding lower profit expectations due to rising fuel costs.

Market breadth remained positive, with advancing stocks outnumbering decliners on both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq, as investors continued to balance optimism over earnings with lingering geopolitical risks.
